testcase.name: test_main_download_video_with_quality
testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_with_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ) as mock_qualities: result = runner.invoke( main, [ "https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "-q", "720", ], ) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result SystemExit(1)>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:21: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_main_download_video_without_quality
testcase.failure:
def test_main_download_video_without_quality(): runner = CliRunner() with patch( "apyrat.apyrat.Downloader._get_available_qualities", return_value=["480", "720", "1080"], ), patch("click.prompt", return_value="720"): result = runner.invoke(main, ["https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I"]) > assert result.exit_code == 0 E assert 1 == 0 E + where 1 = <Result SystemExit(1)>.exit_code
tests/cli_test.py:32: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_quality_available
testcase.failure:
self = <Response [504]>, kwargs = {}
def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any.
:param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """
if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)
try: > return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/requests/models.py:971: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/json/__init__.py:346: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <json.decoder.JSONDecoder object at 0x105047390> s = '504 Gateway Timeout\n' _w = <built-in method match of re.Pattern object at 0x1050258a0>
def decode(self, s, _w=WHITESPACE.match): """Return the Python representation of ``s`` (a ``str`` instance containing a JSON document).
""" obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) end = _w(s, end).end() if end != len(s): > raise JSONDecodeError("Extra data", s, end) E json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 1 column 5 (char 4)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/json/decoder.py:340: JSONDecodeError
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
def test_get_quality_available(): > downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/cli_test.py:36: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ apyrat/apyrat.py:39: in __init__ self.videos = self._get_available_videos() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ apyrat/apyrat.py:61: in _get_available_videos return [self._get_single_video_qualities(self.video_uid)] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ apyrat/apyrat.py:95: in _get_single_video_qualities ).json() ^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <Response [504]>, kwargs = {}
def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any.
:param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """
if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)
try: return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) except JSONDecodeError as e: # Catch JSON-related errors and raise as requests.JSONDecodeError # This aliases json.JSONDecodeError and simplejson.JSONDecodeError > raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) E requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 1 column 5 (char 4)
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/requests/models.py:975: JSONDecodeError

def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any.

def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any.

testcase.failure:
self = <Response [504]>, kwargs = {}

if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)

def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any.
:param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """
if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)
try: return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) except JSONDecodeError as e: # Catch JSON-related errors and raise as requests.JSONDecodeError # This aliases json.JSONDecodeError and simplejson.JSONDecodeError > raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) E requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 1 column 5 (char 4)

mock_wget = <MagicMock name='download' id='4415780048'> mock_isfile = <MagicMock name='isfile' id='4415780816'>
@patch("os.path.isfile", return_value=True) @patch("wget.download") def test_download_when_file_exists(mock_wget, mock_isfile): > downloader = Downloader("https://www.aparat.com/v/qur3I", "outputfile") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

:param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """
